DCL needed: only HR managers should see employees in their own organizational unit. Authorization object P_ORGIN, field ORGEH (org unit). DCL: `where (OrgUnit) = aspect pfcg_auth(P_ORGIN, ORGEH, ACTVT = '03')`. After activation: SELECT returned only employees in logged-in manager's org unit automatically. Tested with 3 different user accounts — each saw only their unit's employees.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: DCL object ka naam CDS view se alag rakhna — linking nahi hota. @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK lagaya hai lekin DCL nahi bana — runtime error: access control object not found. DCL naam bilkul same hona chahiye CDS view se.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does DCL work with OData and Fiori applications?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Fiori app OData se data fetch karta hai. OData service CDS view consume karta hai. CDS view pe DCL hai. Jab Fiori OData call karta hai — user ke context mein — DCL automatically trigger hota hai. OData framework CDS layer se data maangta hai, CDS layer DCL conditions apply karta hai, sirf authorized data return hota hai. Fiori developer ko alag se authorization logic likhne ki zarurat nahi — DCL transparent hai CDS consumers ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a CDS view with DCL is exposed via OData — either through @OData.publish: true or SEGW — the DCL authorization is transparently enforced for OData requests. The OData framework runs under the logged-in user's context, and when it queries the underlying CDS view, the DCL WHERE conditions are automatically applied. The Fiori application developer does not need to implement any authorization filtering — the DCL handles it centrally. This means the same CDS view can be safely exposed to different user groups, with each group seeing only their authorized data subset.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Sales data Fiori app security: CDS view ZCDS_SALES_DATA exposed via OData. DCL restricted by sales organization (VKORG) using authorization object V_VBAK_VKO. Sales manager Germany: OData response shows only German VKORG orders. Sales manager USA: same OData URL, same Fiori app, but response shows only US VKORG orders. DCL works transparently — one app, one CDS view, one OData service, but automatic data isolation per user's authorization.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sochna ki OData security alag se implement karni padti hai jab DCL hai. DCL CDS level pe kaam karta hai — OData consumers ko automatically protection milti hai. Extra OData-level filtering unnecessary hai agar DCL properly defined hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you combine multiple authorization conditions in one DCL?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan — DCL mein multiple conditions combine kar sakte ho. AND aur OR conditions use ho sakti hain. Example: user ke paas VKORG authorization bhi honi chahiye aur VTWEG (distribution channel) bhi. Ya: VKORG ke liye authorization hai OR special override role hai. Complex authorization scenarios ke liye nested conditions bhi possible hain. Har condition pfcg_auth aspect ya literal value comparison ho sakti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DCL supports multiple authorization conditions combined with AND and OR operators. You can require that a user must be authorized for multiple fields simultaneously — for example, both sales organization and distribution channel. Or you can allow access if any one of multiple conditions is met — OR logic for different user types. Conditions can reference different authorization objects in the same DCL. This enables complex, real-world authorization scenarios like: 'User must have VKORG auth AND VTWEG auth' or 'User has CARRID auth OR is a superuser with S_TCODE full access'.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Complex DCL requirement: Airline booking CDS view. Rule 1: Regular agents see only their assigned carrier (S_CARRID, CARRID). Rule 2: Supervisor role (identified by special S_DEVELOP auth object) can see all carriers. DCL combined: `where (CarrierID) = aspect pfcg_auth(S_CARRID, CARRID, ACTVT = '03') OR aspect pfcg_auth(S_DEVELOP, DEVCLASS = '$')`. Supervisors with S_DEVELOP full access see all data. Agents see only their carriers. One DCL handles both user types.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Bahut complex DCL likhna jo maintain karna mushkil ho. Prefer simple DCL with clear authorization logic. Complex scenarios ke liye — multiple DCL roles combine karo (DCL supports role combination) rather than one mega-DCL with complex OR/AND chains.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Multiple conditions in DCL
@MappingRole: true
define role ZCDS_BOOKING_ACCESS {
  grant select on ZCDS_AIRLINE_BOOKING
    " Condition 1: standard agents - carrier restricted
    where (CarrierID, ConnID)
           = aspect pfcg_auth( S_CARRID,
                               CARRID,
                               CONNID,
                               ACTVT = '03' )
    " OR Condition 2: admin users - all access
    or  (BookingClass)
           = aspect pfcg_auth( S_DEVELOP,
                               DEVCLASS,
                               ACTVT = '03' );
}

followupAnswerBank.question: Why does SE16N bypass DCL and what is the alternative?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE16N seedha database table access karta hai — SQL query directly table pe jaata hai. DCL CDS view layer pe kaam karta hai — jab CDS view access hota hai tab DCL filter lagta hai. SE16N mein tum table name type karte ho, CDS view name nahi — isliye CDS layer aur DCL bypass ho jaata hai. Alternative: SE16N access restrict karo sensitive tables ke liye via profile/role. Ya: CDS view SE16N mein by naam access kar sakte ho — phir DCL apply hoga. Ya: custom Fiori app se data dekho jahan DCL protected CDS view access hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE16N in SAP GUI accesses database tables directly — it constructs a direct SELECT on the physical table, completely bypassing the CDS layer and any associated DCL. DCL only activates when a CDS view is accessed — not when the underlying table is queried directly. To protect sensitive data from SE16N bypass: restrict SE16N access via authorization object S_TABU_DIS and table groups for sensitive tables. Alternatively, educate users to access data through CDS-view-based Fiori apps rather than SE16N. In newer SAP systems, you can also access a CDS view name directly in SE16N — in that case the DCL is applied. But table-name access in SE16N always bypasses DCL.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security gap found in audit: Salary data CDS view had DCL restricting to HR users only. But payroll table PA0008 was accessible to developers via SE16N — they could see all salary data directly. Fix implemented: Table authorization group for PA0008 restricted to HR team only via S_TABU_DIS. Now even SE16N shows authorization error for non-HR users on PA0008. DCL protects CDS access; table auth group protects direct table access. Defense in depth.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf DCL lagakar sochna ki data completely secure hai — SE16N aur direct table access routes still open hain. Security ka multi-layer approach chahiye: DCL for CDS consumers, table authorization groups for direct access, profile restrictions for SE16N.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" DCL protects CDS view access
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE
  as select from pa0008
{ key pa0008.pernr, pa0008.lga01, pa0008.bet01 }

" DCL: ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE
@MappingRole: true
define role ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE {
  grant select on ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE
    where (pernr)
           = aspect pfcg_auth( P_PERNR,
                               PSIGN, ACTVT = '03' );
}

" SE16N STILL bypasses above DCL if user types:
" Table Name: PA0008 → direct access, no DCL!

" ABAP: protect direct table access separately
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_TABU_DIS'
  ID 'DICBERCLS' FIELD 'PA'
  ID 'ACTVT'    FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'No authorization for table PA0008' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is inherited access control in CDS views?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Agar tumhara CDS view kisi doosre CDS view pe based hai — composite view ya consumption view — tum parent view ka access control inherit kar sakte ho. `@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT` annotation se child view parent view ki DCL use karta hai. Apna alag DCL likhne ki zarurat nahi — jo parent mein security hai wahi child mein bhi automatically apply hoti hai. Isse maintain karna aasaan hota hai — ek jagah DCL update karo, sab children benefit karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a CDS view is built on top of another CDS view, it can inherit the access control from its base view using @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT. In this case, the consuming view does not need its own DCL — it inherits the row-level security from the view it selects from. This is useful in layered CDS architectures: define DCL once on the interface view, and all consumption views built on it automatically inherit the same access control. This reduces duplication and centralizes security maintenance.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Layered CDS architecture security: ZI_SALES_ORDER (interface view) had DCL restricting by VKORG. Four consumption views built on ZI_SALES_ORDER: ZC_SO_LIST, ZC_SO_DETAIL, ZC_SO_ANALYTICS, ZC_SO_FIORI. Each set @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT. No separate DCL needed for any consumption view. When VKORG authorization rules changed, only the interface view DCL was updated — all four consumption views automatically applied the new rules. Four views, one DCL maintenance point.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT use karna jab base view mein #NOT_REQUIRED set hai — child view bhi unprotected ho jaata hai unintentionally. Hamesha check karo ki base view ka authorization check properly set hai before using inheritance in child views.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Interface view with own DCL
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE
  as select from vbak
{ key vbak.vbeln, vbak.vkorg, vbak.kunnr }

" DCL for interface view (DCL object: ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE)
" ... pfcg_auth on VKORG ...

" Consumption views - inherit security from base
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck:
  #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT
define view ZC_SALES_ORDER_LIST
  as select from ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE
{ vbeln, vkorg, kunnr }  " inherits DCL from base

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck:
  #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT
define view ZC_SALES_ORDER_ANALYTICS
  as select from ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE
{ vkorg, count(*) as OrderCount }  " inherits DCL

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test if DCL is working correctly?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Testing steps: 1) Ek user banao jiske paas limited authorization ho — jaise sirf ek CARRID ke liye S_CARRID auth. 2) Us user se login karo ya su01/SU53 simulate karo. 3) ABAP SELECT FROM cds_view chalao — sirf authorized data aana chahiye. 4) SE16N se same CDS view naam se access karo — DCL apply honi chahiye. 5) SU53 transaction se authorization failures dekho. 6) Eclipse ADT mein 'Run As' different user simulate. ST01 authorization trace bhi use karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing DCL involves verifying that users see only their authorized data subset. Create or use a test user with limited authorizations — for example, only one sales organization in VKORG authorization. Log in as that user or use transaction SU53 to check authorization failures. Run a SELECT FROM the CDS view — the result should contain only data matching the user's authorizations. Compare with a privileged user's result to verify filtering. Use ST01 Authorization Trace to see which authorization objects are being checked at runtime. In Eclipse ADT, the CDS data preview (F8) runs under the logged-in developer's authorizations.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: DCL test scenario in project: DCL written for airline carrier authorization. Testing: created test user ZTESTUSER01 with S_CARRID for only 'LH' (Lufthansa). Logged in as ZTESTUSER01. SELECT FROM ZCDS_AIRLINE_SECURE returned only LH records. ST01 trace confirmed S_CARRID check triggered. Then tested with ZTESTUSER02 having 'AA' and 'LH' — two airlines returned. Admin user (all CARRID auth) — all airlines returned. DCL confirmed working correctly across three user scenarios.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: DCL test karna own admin/developer user se — jo likely has full authorizations. DCL filter nazar nahi aata kyunki sab authorized hai. Always test with a restricted user. Aur: ST01 trace use karna — shows exactly which auth checks are happening.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Testing DCL - ABAP test program
REPORT z_test_dcl_check.

" Run this as a restricted test user
" (not as admin with full authorizations)

" Step 1: Direct SELECT from CDS view
SELECT AirlineID, AirlineName
  FROM zcds_airline_secure
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_authorized).

WRITE: / 'Records visible to current user:',
         lines( lt_authorized ).
LOOP AT lt_authorized INTO DATA(ls_airline).
  WRITE: / ls_airline-AirlineID, ls_airline-AirlineName.
ENDLOOP.

" Step 2: Cross-check - what authorization user has?
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_CARRID'
  ID 'CARRID' FIELD 'LH'
  ID 'ACTVT'  FIELD '03'.
WRITE: / 'LH authorized?', sy-subrc.  " 0 = yes

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the pfcg_auth aspect in DCL and how does it map to PFCG roles?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: pfcg_auth DCL ka built-in function hai jo SAP PFCG (Profile Generator) roles se authorization values read karta hai. Tumhare PFCG role mein authorization object S_CARRID pe CARRID = 'LH' set hai. DCL mein `pfcg_auth(S_CARRID, CARRID, ACTVT = '03')` likhte ho — runtime pe system user ke PFCG role se S_CARRID ke liye authorized CARRID values read karta hai aur unhe DCL WHERE condition mein use karta hai. Matlab: PFCG role maintenance aur DCL ek saath kaam karte hain — business user role manage karta hai access control karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The pfcg_auth aspect in DCL is a built-in function that reads authorization values from the user's PFCG-maintained role assignments at runtime. It references a specific authorization object, maps its authorization fields to CDS view fields, and optionally specifies a fixed activity code like ACTVT = '03' for display. At query time, the system retrieves all values the user is authorized for in the specified authorization object field, and applies them as an IN filter on the corresponding CDS view field. This means authorization maintenance stays in PFCG roles — business admins maintain who sees what without touching DCL code.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Role-based access in practice: Sales order CDS view with DCL using pfcg_auth on V_VBAK_VKO for VKORG. Role ZSALES_GERMANY: VKORG = '1000'. Role ZSALES_USA: VKORG = '2000'. Role ZSALES_ALL: VKORG = '*' (all). Assign roles to users in SU01/PFCG. User with ZSALES_GERMANY sees only VKORG 1000 orders. No DCL change needed to add a new VKORG — just update the PFCG role. Clean separation: DCL defines the rule, PFCG maintains the values.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: PFCG role mein authorization values maintain nahi karna — DCL pfcg_auth se koi value nahi milti, user kuch bhi nahi dekh pata. PFCG role maintenance aur DCL setup dono parallel mein karne chahiye. Test: user ke role mein sahi authorization values hain ki nahi — SU53 se verify karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" PFCG authorization setup (maintained in SU01/PFCG):
" Authorization Object: V_VBAK_VKO
"   Field VKORG: 1000 (sales org Germany)
"   Field ACTVT: 03 (display)

" DCL maps pfcg_auth to CDS view field
@MappingRole: true
define role ZCDS_SALES_ORDER_DCL {
  grant select on ZCDS_SALES_ORDER_SECURE
    where (SalesOrg, DistChannel)
           = aspect pfcg_auth(
               V_VBAK_VKO,  " auth object
               VKORG,       " maps to SalesOrg field
               VTWEG,       " maps to DistChannel field
               ACTVT = '03' " fixed activity - display
             );
}
" Runtime: reads user's PFCG V_VBAK_VKO auth values
" Applies as: WHERE SalesOrg IN ('1000') AND DistChannel IN ('10')
